intro: Claytonia cachinalensis, also known as Cachinal, is a plant species in the purslane family (Portulacaceae) native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate conditions such as oxidative stress and infection. The bioactive constituents of Claytonia cachinalensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional folk medicine, Claytonia cachinalensis is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
